title: AD Taylor-tower P12: sparse high-order tensors -- colored GUW mixed-partial recovery (sparse Hessian via distance-2 coloring + reverse-over-Taylor Hv; CSR storage)
goal: Exploit sparsity in GUW high-order recovery per docs/design/AD_TAYLOR_TOWER.md sec P12. Build a variable-interaction graph (user-supplied hyperedge pattern OR runtime probe), select seed directions by greedy distance-2 graph coloring, and recover the full sparse symmetric Hessian in #colors reverse-over-Taylor Hessian-vector products (H*w_c per color class) rather than the m unit-direction passes a dense Hessian-vector recovery needs -- so the AD pass count is driven by interaction bandwidth, not by m. Store CSR-like (row-ptr/col-idx/values). Provide a general order>=3 sparse-mixed-partials API (block decomposition + principal-lattice GUW recovery). Conservative structure detection with dense fallback as the safety net. New files only: no edits to lib/core/runtime_taylor.c, lib/backend/autodiff_codegen.cpp, or lib/core/ad/guw.esk.
acceptance: sparse-hessian matches DENSE recovery (uncompressed m-column Hv) AND closed-form analytic Hessian to abs/rel < 1e-10 on banded partially-separable f (polynomial + transcendental), m=4..6, for BOTH probed and explicit-pattern structure
savings: for m=5,10,20 banded, #colors (= sparse AD directions) stays flat (bandwidth-driven, =3 for a path graph) vs dense = m directions; printed table with real numbers
dense fallback: a fully-dense f ((sum x)^2) is detected (colors>=m or density>0.6) and recovered via the dense path, matching the analytic Hessian exactly, dense? flag = #t
CSR accessors (row-ptr/col-idx/values), sparse-hessian-ref, sparse-hessian-nonzeros consistent and symmetric
regressions hold (0 regressions): guw_multivariate_test 35/35, scripts/run_ad_oracle.sh 56/56, scripts/run_sicp_smoke.sh 88/88
new test tests/ad/sparse_tensors_test.esk PASS under BOTH -r and AOT and wired into CTest (sparse_tensors_runtime_smoke)
campaign_payoff: Sparse Hessians of partially-separable functions (banded/stencil/ML-block structure) are recovered in O(bandwidth) reverse-over-Taylor passes independent of dimension m, versus the O(m) (dense Hv) or O(C(m+d-1,d)) (dense GUW) the P4 layer pays -- the P12 compression result. Uses only the P5-proven order-1 reverse-over-Taylor Hessian-vector product plus a graph-coloring seed-selection layer, all as a new self-contained Scheme module over the existing tower arithmetic with zero kernel/codegen edits. CSR storage makes the result directly consumable by sparse linear solvers.

delivered.module: lib/core/ad/sparse_guw.esk (new core AD module, require core.ad.sparse_guw). Fully self-contained (own list/vector/graph helpers; no stdlib map/for-each/assoc; and deliberately does NOT (require core.ad.guw) -- mixing the taylor special form with the derivative-n/gradient tower forms in one compilation unit corrupts the tower codegen, so the whole module stays within the derivative-n family). Provides sparse-hessian, sparse-hessian-pat, sparse-hessian-ref, sparse-hessian-nonzeros, sparse-hessian-{row-ptr,col-idx,values}, sparse-hessian-colors, sparse-hessian-directions, sparse-hessian-dense?, sparse-mixed-partials, sparse-mixed-partials-ref, sparse-mixed-partials-entries.
delivered.sparse_hessian: SHIPPED design = distance-2 coloring + reverse-over-Taylor Hv. (1) Interaction graph G on {0..m-1}: from an explicit hyperedge pattern (clique per hyperedge; always safe, caller asserts ground truth) or by PROBING -- the full dense Hessian (via m unit-direction Hv passes) at xs and at 2 deterministic GLSL-hash pseudo-random nearby points (no OS randomness; OR across probes reduces coincidental-cancellation false negatives; honest heuristic, not a proof). (2) Greedy DISTANCE-2 coloring of G: order vertices by descending degree, forbid colors of distance-<=2 colored vertices, assign smallest free -- a valid star coloring on banded/path/arrowhead graphs, sufficient for DIRECT one-shot recovery (no substitution). (3) Per color class C_c, indicator direction w_c, and recover the whole vector H*w_c in ONE pass via the P5 composition (gradient (lambda (x) (derivative-n (lambda (t) (f (x + t*w_c))) 0.0 1)) xs). Distance-2 validity => each nonzero (H*w_c)_v maps to exactly one unknown: diag[v] (v in C_c) or the single off-diagonal H_{v,u} (unique adjacent u in C_c). Both-sides cross-check (read H_{v,u} from color(u) and from color(v)); disagreement > 1e-8 triggers dense fallback. If coloring degenerates (colors >= m OR edge-density > 0.6, i.e. dense f) an explicit DENSE FALLBACK recovers all m columns via unit Hv passes. Result stored CSR-like (row-ptr length m+1, col-idx sorted ascending per row, values; diagonal always present).
delivered.why_Hv_not_hessian_builtin: The dense reference/probe/fallback use m unit-direction Hv passes rather than the built-in (hessian f xs): mixing the built-in hessian and the reverse-over-Taylor gradient in one compilation unit trips a whole-program type-inference quirk that mis-types the hessian result as a scalar. Routing every dense computation through the single sg-hvp primitive keeps the module self-consistent and immune. The order-1 Hv is robust to large m (validated to m=20).
delivered.sparse_mixed_partials: order>=3 API implemented as block decomposition + self-contained principal-lattice GUW recovery reading each directional coefficient through derivative-n (n!*c_n / n!): for each hyperedge/block of k variables the block's dense order-n sub-tensor is recovered (N=C(n+k-1,k-1) directions, N x N Gaussian solve with partial pivoting, same math as guw.esk but derivative-n-based and independent of it) and re-embedded into full length-m multi-indices, unioned across blocks; support(beta) not contained in any block is asserted structurally zero. sparse-mixed-partials-ref(sp, idxs) takes variable indices with repetition like guw's mixed-partial.
delivered.tests: tests/ad/sparse_tensors_test.esk: 43 checks -- quadratic banded (analytic Hii=2/H_{i,i+1}=1/else 0 + dense-recovery cross-check, probed AND explicit-pattern, m=4,5,6); transcendental banded (Sum xi^2 xi+1 + sin(xi xi+1)) vs dense recovery m=4,5; dense fallback (Sum xi)^2 m=4 (dense? flag, colors=m, all entries 2, analytic + dense match); savings table m=5,10,20 (colors flat = 3 vs dense = m); CSR accessor + symmetry sanity. Prints PASS: on success, FAIL:/exit 1 on failure. Registered in CMakeLists.txt as sparse_tensors_runtime_smoke (JIT -r, PASS/FAIL regex). Green under BOTH -r and AOT.

remainder: ORDER >= 3 sparse-mixed-partials is IMPLEMENTED and algorithmically verified in isolation (raw derivative-n order-3 directional recovery reproduces integer polynomial partials in a minimal program) but is NOT exercised in the gating test and is not usable at runtime through this module as loaded: a PRE-EXISTING upstream codegen fragility means that once a module using order-1 derivative-n/gradient is compiled, order->=2 derivative-n on transcendentals mis-evaluates (returns 0) or faults, and high-order multivariate towers crash deterministically at vector length >= 4 on many function/program shapes. This is the SAME limitation that gates core.ad.guw itself to dimension <= 3 (hand-tuned functions). Fixing it requires editing lib/core/runtime_taylor.c / lib/backend/autodiff_codegen.cpp, which are OUT OF SCOPE for P12 (new-files-only). Tracked as a follow-up engine phase. The order-2 sparse-hessian path is IMMUNE (order-1 towers only) and is the fully-gated P12 deliverable.

Coloring is greedy distance-2 (symmetric) -- a valid star coloring on the tested banded/path/arrowhead graphs, sufficient for direct recovery; NOT the Gebremedhin-Manne-Pothen minimal-star-coloring (which additionally needs substitution-based recovery). Direction-count minimality beyond distance-2 validity is not a P12 gate.

Probed structure detection is a documented heuristic (3-point OR of dense-Hv probes); the both-sides cross-check plus dense fallback are the correctness safety net. An explicit pattern skips probing and is always safe.
Savings comparison is stated as sparse #colors vs dense #unit-Hv-directions (= m), the apples-to-apples reverse-over-Taylor cost; not versus dense-GUW's C(m+1,2) (which would show a larger ratio).

verifications.evidence: tests/ad/sparse_tensors_test.esk 43/43 PASS under BOTH -r (JIT, ctest sparse_tensors_runtime_smoke 4.72s) and AOT (exit 0). Correctness: quadratic banded m=4,5,6 -- analytic H00=2, H_{0,1}=1, H_{0,2}=0, symmetric; sparse (probed AND explicit-pattern) == dense unit-Hv recovery max|diff|=0. Transcendental banded m=4,5 (sin) sparse == dense recovery max|diff| < 1e-10. Dense fallback (sum x)^2 m=4: dense?=#t, colors=4, all H_ij=2 == analytic == dense recovery. SAVINGS (banded, distance-2 coloring of the path graph): m=5 colors=3 dirs=3 vs dense 5 (1.67x); m=10 colors=3 vs 10 (3.33x); m=20 colors=3 vs 20 (6.67x) -- colors FLAT in m (bandwidth-driven), correctness holds at scale. CSR accessors consistent + symmetric. Regressions (0 regressions): guw_multivariate_test 35/35 (ctest 6.34s); scripts/run_ad_oracle.sh total=56 passed=56 failed=0 crashed=0 hung=0 gate=PASS; scripts/run_sicp_smoke.sh 88/88 (0 xfail, 0 XPASS). No existing AD file modified (only CMakeLists.txt add_test) -- order<=2 gradient path and the guw/P4 layer untouched. Order-2 sparse-hessian is the fully-gated deliverable; order>=3 sparse-mixed-partials implemented + documented but blocked live by a pre-existing high-order-tower codegen fragility (out-of-scope engine fix).
